tasks.title: Modularize lib.rs and tighten visibility
evidence: lib.rs is a flat list of 25+ pub mod exports, creating an unnecessarily large public API surface.
Many exported modules are command-specific and will be consolidated by RQ-0294.
Integration tests in crates/ralph/tests/ rely on these exports, making tightening visibility a delicate task.
The file lacks logical grouping for its 30+ modules.
plan: Audit lib.rs exports against main.rs and tests/ usage.
Categorize modules into logical groups (Core, Commands, UI, Utils) within lib.rs using comments or sub-modules.
Tighten visibility to pub(crate) for modules not required by the public API or integration tests.
Coordinate with RQ-0294 to consolidate command modules into a single pub mod commands entry.
Verify changes with make ci and all integration tests.
notes: This task should follow RQ-0294 to avoid redundant path changes.
Consider using pub(crate) for modules that must be visible to the test suite if they can be moved into the same crate structure, though integration tests require pub.

tasks.title: Improve fsutil.rs cohesion
evidence: fsutil.rs (447 lines) mixes locking logic (9+ functions, 2 structs) with temp cleanup and atomic write helpers.
Locking logic requires specific Unix-only dependencies (libc) and complex error formatting that obscures the core utility functions.
Widespread usage of locking logic across CLI and TUI modules suggests it should be a first-class module.
plan: Create crates/ralph/src/lock.rs and move locking-related structs (DirLock, LockOwner) and functions (acquire_dir_lock, is_supervising_process) there.
Move lock-specific private helpers (parse_lock_owner, write_lock_owner, read_lock_owner, pid_is_running, format_lock_error, is_supervising_label, command_line) to lock.rs.
Expose the new module in crates/ralph/src/lib.rs.
Update all call sites (queue.rs, cli/task.rs, tui/mod.rs, etc.) to import from the new lock module.
Split or update locking tests in crates/ralph/tests/fsutil_test.rs and other integration tests.
Run `make ci` to verify the refactoring.
notes: Consider if general fs helpers like sync_dir_best_effort should be moved to a more central location if used by both lock and fsutil.
The goal is to reach a state where fsutil.rs is strictly for stateless filesystem operations.

tasks.title: Standardize error handling context
evidence: crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/process.rs contains sparse error messages like 'lock ctrl-c state' and 'stdout reader panicked' which lack actionable context.
crates/ralph/src/queue/validation.rs demonstrates the desired pattern of descriptive, actionable error messages with clear fix suggestions.
crates/ralph/src/runner.rs uses generic RunnerError::Other mapping that often hides the underlying cause or domain context.
Error handling in crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/ doesn't consistently use the Context trait to include task IDs in failure messages.
plan: Audit crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/process.rs and runners.rs for sparse or generic error messages and replace with descriptive Context.
Audit crates/ralph/src/runner.rs for RunnerError::Other usages and wrap with domain-specific context (e.g., 'executing codex runner').
Refactor crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/ to use anyhow::Context for including task IDs in all task-related failure paths.
Ensure all validation errors follow the high-quality pattern established in validation.rs (actionable + descriptive).
Verify improved context by manually triggering failures (e.g., missing binaries, invalid IDs) and inspecting output.
Run `make ci` to ensure all error paths are correctly typed and handleable.
notes: Use crates/ralph/src/queue/validation.rs as the gold standard for error context quality.
The goal is to ensure every error bubble-up to the CLI includes enough context for a user to diagnose without reading source code.

tasks.title: Centralize prompt organization
evidence: Current prompt loading logic is duplicated across 7+ modules in crates/ralph/src/prompts_internal/.
Each module manually defines relative paths, embedded defaults, and boilerplate load/render functions.
Hardcoded string literals for template keys (e.g., {{TASK_ID}}, {{PROJECT_TYPE_GUIDANCE}}) are scattered and inconsistently handled.
The modularization of prompts has introduced redundant code that could be unified via a central registry.
plan: Define a `Prompt` enum in `prompts_internal/util.rs` (or `mod.rs`) that maps each prompt to its relative path and embedded content.
Implement a `load` method for the `Prompt` enum that handles the fallback logic consistently.
Add a unified `render` method or helper that handles common variable expansion (env/config) and project guidance injection.
Refactor all submodules in `crates/ralph/src/prompts_internal/` to use the new `Prompt` enum for loading.
Update prompt consumers (phases, task builder, scan) to leverage the centralized registry.
Standardize placeholder validation and escaping across all prompt types.
Run `make ci` to verify that all prompts still load and render correctly.
notes: Centralizing prompts into an enum will make it easier to add new prompt types and metadata in the future.
Consider if the enum should also track which placeholders are required for each prompt type.

tasks.title: Audit and tighten public API surface
evidence: lib.rs currently exports 25+ submodules as `pub`, many of which are implementation details (e.g., `prompts`, `runner`, `run_cmd`).
Within these submodules, many items (structs, functions, enums) are marked `pub` when they are only consumed within the crate or even within their parent module.
Specific examples found in `crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/runners.rs` and `crates/ralph/src/prompts_internal/review.rs` where public items can be restricted to `pub(crate)` or `pub(super)`.
plan: Wait for RQ-0296 (Modularize lib.rs and tighten visibility) to complete its reorganization of lib.rs.
Perform a recursive audit of all items marked `pub` within crates/ralph/src/ submodules.
Restrict visibility to `pub(crate)` or `pub(super)` for all items not required by the public API or integration tests in `tests/`.
Consolidate public exports into a minimal, intentional API surface documented in lib.rs.
Run `make ci` to verify that integration tests still have sufficient access.
notes: This task should follow RQ-0296 to avoid redundant path changes during module reorganization.
Integration tests in `tests/` are the primary constraint; consider moving some logic-specific tests into internal `#[cfg(test)]` blocks to allow further tightening.

tasks.title: Achieve feature parity across all runners
evidence: Claude runner exposes unique flags like --verbose and --permission-mode that are not consistently mapped or available for other runners.
Gemini uses a hardcoded --approval-mode yolo which should be configurable or standardized.
Resume flags vary significantly across runners (resume vs -s vs --resume).
Output format selection is inconsistent (--json vs --format json vs --output-format stream-json).
Streaming consistency is not guaranteed when interleaved with non-JSON output, particularly in Codex and OpenCode.
plan: Audit all runner CLI capabilities for shared features such as verbose mode and approval/permission settings.
Incorporate shared flag support into the RunnerCommandBuilder proposed by RQ-0297.
Refactor run_with_streaming_json in process.rs to handle varied JSON output styles and interleaved logs consistently.
Expose standardized settings (e.g., verbose, approval_mode) in AgentConfig and propagate them to runners.
Verify parity using integration tests that simulate diverse runner CLI outputs and edge cases.
Update documentation to reflect the standardized runner interface.
notes: This task is a logical follow-up to the DRY refactoring in RQ-0297.
Improved parsing logic from RQ-0299 will be foundational for consistent streaming.

tasks.title: Audit and split contracts.rs
evidence: crates/ralph/src/contracts.rs is 618 lines long (as of Jan 26, 2026).
It mixes configuration structures (AgentConfig, QueueConfig), task definitions (Task, TaskStatus), and queue file structures (QueueFile).
Violation of 'Cohesion' principle: it handles too many distinct responsibilities in a single file.
Violation of 'Size heuristic': it exceeds the recommended ~400 LOC.
plan: Create crates/ralph/src/contracts/ directory.
Extract configuration-related structs and enums to crates/ralph/src/contracts/config.rs.
Extract task-related structs and enums to crates/ralph/src/contracts/task.rs.
Extract queue file structures to crates/ralph/src/contracts/queue.rs.
Move associated Default implementations and unit tests to their respective new modules.
Update crates/ralph/src/contracts/mod.rs to host re-exports for all items to maintain backward compatibility.
Replace crates/ralph/src/contracts.rs with a file that simply re-exports the contracts module or remove it if using contracts/mod.rs.
Run `make ci` to verify implementation correctness and ensure no regressions.
notes: This refactor will improve maintainability and make it easier to locate specific data structures.
By maintaining re-exports in contracts.rs (or contracts/mod.rs), we avoid a massive rename across the entire codebase.

tasks.title: Audit and improve CLI help examples
evidence: Audit of `crates/ralph/src/cli/` reveals that while many commands have good examples, some gaps remain.
`TaskCommand::Update` in `task.rs` lacks explicit `--rp-on`/`--rp-off` examples in its `after_long_help` block.
`RunCommand::Loop` in `run.rs` lacks `--rp-on`/`--rp-off` examples, unlike `RunCommand::One`.
`RunCommand::One` examples for `--phases` are present but could be more descriptive about what each phase does (Plan/Implement/Review).
Most `queue` commands are well-documented, but `ralph queue validate` and `ralph queue next-id` have very minimal single-line examples.
plan: Review and audit all `after_long_help` blocks in `crates/ralph/src/cli/` submodules.
Add `--rp-on` and `--rp-off` examples to `TaskCommand::Update` in `crates/ralph/src/cli/task.rs`.
Add `--rp-on` and `--rp-off` examples to `RunCommand::Loop` in `crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs`.
Expand `--phases` examples in `run.rs` to clarify the 3-phase, 2-phase, and 1-phase workflows.
Enhance minimal examples for utility commands like `queue validate` and `queue next-id`.
Run `cargo run -p ralph -- <command> --help` to verify all help texts look polished and consistent.
Run `make ci` to ensure no regressions.
notes: Focus on making examples actionable and covering newer flags like `--rp-on`, `--rp-off`, and `--phases` consistently.
Ensure examples match the actual behavior described in `docs/cli.md`.

tasks.title: Optimize TUI filtering and indexing
evidence: rebuild_filtered_view_with_preferred in crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs (moved from mod.rs by RQ-0292) reconstructs an O(N) HashMap of all tasks on every call to resolve indices.
rebuild_filtered_view is called on nearly every TUI event (18 call sites in mod.rs), even when filter state hasn't changed (e.g., status message updates).
Queue filtering logic in crates/ralph/src/queue/search.rs returns Vec<&Task>, which is efficient for references but the TUI then performs redundant work to map these back to indices.
plan: Cache the task ID to index mapping in the App struct and only update it when the queue is reloaded or mutated.
Introduce a filters_dirty flag to the App struct to skip redundant re-filtering when the filter parameters (query, status, tags, scopes) remain unchanged.
Refactor rebuild_filtered_view to leverage the cached mapping and avoid rebuilding the HashMap.
Profile TUI performance with a synthetic queue of 1,000+ tasks to identify further latency sources in the render/update loop.
Run `make ci` to ensure no regressions in TUI behavior or filtering logic.
notes: This optimization is crucial for maintaining TUI responsiveness as project history grows.
Consider if filter_tasks should return indices directly to further simplify the TUI's responsibilities.

tasks.title: Standardize timestamp handling
evidence: Direct usage of OffsetDateTime::parse(..., &Rfc3339) exists in validation.rs, reports.rs, prune.rs, edit.rs, repair.rs, and validate.rs.
OffsetDateTime::now_utc() is used directly in reports.rs and prune.rs instead of using timeutil.
timeutil.rs lacks centralized parsing and formatting helpers for OffsetDateTime, leading to duplicated 'time' crate imports across modules.
Consistent subsecond precision (9 digits) is defined in timeutil.rs but not enforced during parsing or used in all formatting sites.
plan: Extend crates/ralph/src/timeutil.rs with `parse_rfc3339(ts: &str) -> Result<OffsetDateTime>` and `format_rfc3339(dt: OffsetDateTime) -> Result<String>` using the fixed precision format.
Refactor all direct OffsetDateTime::parse calls to use timeutil::parse_rfc3339.
Refactor direct OffsetDateTime::now_utc() calls to use timeutil helpers where the intent is to generate or format a queue-compatible timestamp.
Centralize 'time' and 'Rfc3339' imports within timeutil.rs and remove redundant imports from consumer modules.
Update tests to verify that all timestamps generated by the system adhere to the fixed 9-digit subsecond precision.
Run `make ci` to ensure no regressions in queue validation or reporting.
notes: Moving parse_ts from reports.rs to timeutil.rs is a key step.
The goal is to ensure the entire system uses the same RFC3339 flavor with consistent precision.
